business.headline: Small teams will soon disrupt big companies in AI era: Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, the founder of OpenExO and founding executive director of Singularity University said rapid advances in AI are changing how companies operate and compete.

The next $1 trillion company could have only 5 employees: Singularity's Salim IsmailSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Salim Ismail, founding executive director of Singularity University and founder of OpenExO, said the rapid acceleration of technologies such as artificial intelligence is changing the economics of innovation and giving small teams unprecedented power to disrupt large corporations.

India a bright spot amid global uncertainty, says AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large population, strong domestic demand and long-term infrastructure needs make it one of the most promising markets globally, says Arvind Tiku.
Renewables, urbanisation and demand to power India's growth: AT Capital's Arvind TikuIndia's large domestic market and young population create strong long-term growth potential, says Arvind Tiku.

If valuables vanish from your locker, can insurance cover the loss?This is the second story in Lock & Key, a series examining how India's bank locker system works and what customers should know before trusting their valuables to a steel vault.

Either I collapse or get up and go: Anil Agarwal reflects on loss of his sonVedanta chairman Anil Agarwal said the death of his son Agnivesh has strengthened his resolve to continue working for India, urging opportunities for youth and calling for world-class education at home.

ED arrests 2 in Rs 183-crore online ponzi scam; accused returned from DubaiThe case was registered in December 2024 in Thane, accusing Sudhir Kotadiya, Umang Kotadiya, Jaysukh Sakhariya and others of running a fraudulent digital investment scheme that allegedly collected large sums from investors in Gujarat and Maharashtra.

Live: Two vessels carrying 92,700 metric tons of LPG en route to India, govt assuresLPG Crisis Live Updates: Amid reports of commercial LPG cylinder shortage, Karnataka minister Priyank Kharge slammed the Centre, calling the crisis a result of the BJP government's "disastrous policy" and questioned PM Narendra Modi's foreign policy moves. Meanwhile, the shortage is beginning to affect services on the ground as well—food for the public at the "Didi Ki Rasoi" run by Jeevika didis at Rohtas Sadar Hospital has been stopped due to lack of LPG cylinders, and patient meals could also be affected if supply is not restored.

Which four shipping routes control global trade? Karan Adani breaks it downKaran Adani explained that a large part of global shipping passes through four critical choke points. These routes act as key gateways for international trade and energy transport.

How is the global energy landscape changing? Karan Adani explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Karan Adani said the world has witnessed repeated disruptions in supply chains over the past few years, forcing countries to rethink how they manage energy and trade.

After Air India, IndiGo imposes fuel surcharge amid Middle East crisisThe energy crisis triggered by the ongoing war in the Middle East is all set to make your air travel costlier. India's biggest domestic airline, IndiGo, on Friday announced it will impose Rs 425 to Rs 2,300 fuel surcharge on domestic and international flights from Friday (March 14).

Energy, ports and materials define Adani Group's strategy: Karan AdaniKaran Adani emphasised that the group's business expansion is not random but guided by long-term economic changes. Energy transition, digital infrastructure and industrial materials are areas expected to grow significantly in the coming years.

Adani Ports ready to handle energy cargo amid global disruptions: Karan AdaniSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Karan Adani, Managing Director of Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd said supply chains worldwide have faced repeated shocks in recent years, forcing countries to rethink their dependence on highly globalised trade networks.

In an AI-driven world, storytelling and creativity will matter most: Christina WallaceWallace spoke about the growing impact of artificial intelligence. While AI will change many jobs, she believes human creativity will remain essential.

Storytelling will be the most valuable skill in the AI era: Christina WallaceOne skill that Wallace believes will become even more important in the age of AI is storytelling. While machines can produce data and structured information, they struggle to capture human emotion and experiences.

IndiGo, Air India seek tax relief as airspace bans hit operations, raise costIndiGo and Air India have urged the government to cut aviation fuel taxes and reduce private airport charges as the Iran conflict and Pakistan airspace ban force longer routes, raising operational costs and straining airline finances.
India's bond market must expand to finance Viksit Bharat goals, says Anurag MittalIndia will require massive investment to sustain growth, says Mittal.

India needs stronger bond market to fund Viksit Bharat vision, says Anurag MittalMittal explained that while India's growth story has been strong, future development will need long-term capital from a wider range of investors, not just government spending.

LPG production up by 30%, avoid panic buying: Govt amid shortage fearsAddressing concerns over reports of shortages in some areas, Sujatha Sharma, Joint Secretary in the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, said LPG production has been increased by around 30% since March 5 to meet rising demand.

What's driving gold's rally? World Gold Council's Sachin Jain explainsSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026, Sachin Jain, Regional CEO (India) at the World Gold Council, said the factors supporting gold prices remain strong.

entertainment.headline: Akshay Kumar cites Dhurandhar to explain Bollywood's changing trends Bollywood actor Akshay Kumar highlighted the evolving trends in Indian cinema, noting the renewed popularity of high-energy action films like Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar. He emphasised how audience preferences cycle through genres, with current favour leaning towards action and horror comedies.

Akshay Kumar reveals why dinner talk with wife Twinkle has moved beyond politics At the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Akshay Kumar spoke about dinner table conversation at home with wife Twinkle Khanna. The actor shared their discussions no longer don't include politics, and why.

Flow like water: Akshay Kumar talks about surviving Bollywood's highs and lows Akshay Kumar shared insights on his disciplined approach to managing four films a year and maintaining high energy through effective time management. He also spoke about his early morning routine and love for comedy.

He is gold: KJo impressed by Anil Kapoor's performance in Subedaar. See post Karan Johar gave a shout-out to Anil Kapoor's performance in the recent release Subedaar. The movie is currently streaming on Prime Video.

Akshay Kumar joins Golmaal 5; sports bald new look in BTS clip. See here Akshay Kumar announced his entry into the Golmaal franchise with the upcoming fifth instalment in a 'bald' new avatar.  The actor shared a photo with a major update from the film's sets. Read all about it.

Lyricist-poet Vairamuthu wins Jnanpith Award, third Tamil recipient in 20 years Acclaimed poet and lyricist Vairamuthu has been awarded the Jnanpith Award for his contribution to Indian literature. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin shared a post on social media and congratulated the stalwart.

There can be only one Aamir: Saira Banu's touching note for actor on 61st birthday Veteran actor Saira Banu posted a heartfelt message for Aamir Khan on his birthday, highlighting his personal bond with her family and longstanding admiration for Dilip Kumar.

Farah Khan reveals reason why she once avoided working with Akshaye Khanna Farah Khan shared that she once avoided working with Akshaye Khanna in the 1990s. Read here what she said, and the opinion she arrived at after they collaborated on the 2001 hit, Dil Chahta Hai.

Watch: Alia-Ranbir enjoy family time with Raha in Hong Kong park Alia Bhatt is set to celebrate her 33rd birthday with Ranbir Kapoor and Raha in Hong Kong. Several videos and pictures of the trio enjoying family time in a park have emerged online.

Don't call us foreigners: Arunachal girl band Gilithigreams on outsiders label Gilithigreams, a girl pop band from Arunachal Pradesh, regaled guests at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, and urged people not to address Northeasterners as foreigners.

Sunidhi Chauhan postpones Kolkata concert on medical advice. Read statement Sunidhi Chauhan has postponed her Kolkata show, citing a throat infection. In a post on social media, she said her doctors have advised her to rest.

Rajkummar Rao and Keerthy Suresh team up for start-up drama Raftaar, release date out Rajkummar Rao and Keerthy Suresh have announced their upcoming film Raftaar, which is set to release in theatres on July 24, 2026.

Anurag Dobhal's health worsens after return from ICU. Pray for him, says manager Anurag Dobhal's condition has reportedly worsened after his return from ICU, where he was admitted following a car crash. His manager confirmed that the YouTuber's condition has become complicated, and he remains under medical supervision.

Sri Lankan MP Namal Rajapaksa names Vijay as favourite actor Sri Lankan MP Namal Rajapaksa shared at the India Today Conclave 2026 that Vijay was his favourite actor. He said he plans to watch Vijay's film with his father.

Singer Amit Jadhav claims Saiyaara copied his short film Khwabon, seeks credit Singer Amit Jadhav has alleged that the 2025 film Saiyaara is similar to his short film Khwabon and said he did not receive any response from Yash Raj Films despite reaching out.

Dhurandhar 2: Ranveer Singh's film opens for advance bookings Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge opened advance bookings for its first day across cinemas in India on March 14, ahead of its March 19 release.

RGV says industry is waiting for Aditya Dhar, Yami Gautam to fumble and fall Ram Gopal Varma praised Aditya Dhar for transforming the grammar of storytelling in Indian cinema with Dhurandhar. He also claimed the director's success has made many in the industry envious.

Bhumi Pednekar praises Dhurandhar, wants to work with Aditya Dhar, Ranveer Singh Bhumi Pednekar shared that watching Dhurandhar made her eager to collaborate with filmmaker Aditya Dhar and actor Ranveer Singh in the future.

How Netflix's One Piece live action is good, but not quite the same as the anime Netflix's One Piece live action stays faithful to the story, but condensing 40 to 50 anime episodes into an eight-episode season makes the vast, quirky world feel different from its anime counterpart.

general news.headline: Why Khamenei didn't go into bunker? Iran Supreme Leader's aide explainsAyatollah Ali Khamenei refused to leave his Tehran home or move to a bunker despite security concerns, insisting he would do so only if safe shelters were provided for all 19 million residents of the city, his aide said at the India Today Conclave.

Trump calls on allies to send warships, says Hormuz will open one way or otherIn a statement, Donald Trump said several countries affected by the crisis in the Strait of Hormuz were preparing to send warships to operate alongside the United States in order to keep the vital shipping corridor open.

Why Iran opened Strait of Hormuz for Indian oil? Iranian envoy explainsIranian Ambassador Mohammad Fathali, speaking at the India Today Conclave, said some Indian vessels have been allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, but refused to say if all oil tankers would be given safe passage.

When Khamenei helped build a hospital in India after death of pregnant womanDecades before he became Iran's most powerful figure, Ali Khamenei travelled through India as a young cleric. In 1980s, at the age of 41, he visited Karnataka and Kashmir in the early years after Iran's Islamic Revolution.

Persona-cracies: Kalli Purie on strong personalities reshaping democracySpeaking about the strain on democratic systems, she described the emergence of what she called "persona-cracies" — a phase where the "big personalities of leaders" begin to eclipse the institutions meant to contain them.

Love jihad victim: Kumbh girl Monalisa's father seeks Madhya Pradesh CM's helpMonalisa Bhonsle, who gained fame during the Prayagraj Kumbh Mela, married Farman Khan in Kerala, triggering a controversy after her father alleged she was a victim of "love jihad" and sought Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav's intervention.

Meet Maryam Mirzakhani, Iranian who became first woman to win Nobel Prize of mathsMaryam Mirzakhani grew up in Tehran dreaming of becoming a writer, not a mathematician. Yet she went on to win the Fields Medal in 2014, becoming the first and only woman till date to receive the world's highest mathematics honour. Her life remains one of the most inspiring stories in modern science.

Get married, bhai: Indian cricketers jealous of Abhishek's fandom, jokes SamsonIndian cricketers were slightly jealous of Abhishek Sharma's popularity, joked Sanju Samson at the India Today Conclave 2026. At the event, Samson engaged in some hilarious leg-pulling, asking Abhishek to get married soon.

Flow like water: Akshay Kumar talks about surviving Bollywood's highs and lowsAkshay Kumar shared insights on his disciplined approach to managing four films a year and maintaining high energy through effective time management. He also spoke about his early morning routine and love for comedy.

India did all to help: Iran envoy ends debate on sinking of IRIS DenaThe sinking of the IRIS Dena, which was returning from a naval exercise in Visakhapatnam, by a US submarine near Sri Lanka triggered a legal and moral debate. Iran's envoy Mohammad Fathali has now put an end to the debate with his remarks at the India Today Conclave 2026.

Will US deploy boots on ground in Iran's Kharg Island? What Israel envoy saidSpeaking at the India Today Conclave 2026 in New Delhi, Israel's Ambassador to India, Reuven Azar said any decision to deploy US troops in Iran for ground operations would depend on the US President.

Live: Iran says Hormuz closed only to enemy vessels, threatens to attack US energy sitesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: As the war enters its 15th day, regional tensions in the Middle East intensified as fresh strikes and security incidents were reported across multiple countries. In Lebanon, local authorities said the death toll from recent Israeli attacks has climbed to 773. Israel also issued an evacuation warning for an industrial zone in Iran's Tabriz, signalling the possibility of imminent strikes amid ongoing missile and drone exchanges. In Iraq, a missile struck a helipad inside the US Embassy compound in Baghdad's heavily fortified Green Zone, with smoke seen rising from the complex. Meanwhile, rockets and missiles launched toward northern Israel were either intercepted or landed in open areas in the Upper Galilee region. Iran's Fars News Agency reported strikes on military facilities on Kharg Island, including a maritime base and helicopter infrastructure, warning of potential retaliation if oil sites are targeted. In Dubai, debris from an interception hit a building façade without causing injuries. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

RGV says industry is waiting for Aditya Dhar, Yami Gautam to fumble and fallRam Gopal Varma praised Aditya Dhar for transforming the grammar of storytelling in Indian cinema with Dhurandhar. He also claimed the director's success has made many in the industry envious.

Borrowed money for food: Iran Supreme Leader's aide on Mojtaba's simple lifeAbdul Majid Hakeem Ilahi, the aide of Mojtaba Khamenei, said that the selection of Khamenei as the next Supreme Leader did not come as a dynastic practice; it was instead a decision taken by the Constitutional Council, whose members are elected by the people of Iran.

Pak's biggest export is terrorism, US shouldn't cosy up to it: Laura LoomerAmid warming of ties between the US and Pakistan, far-right activist and staunch Donald Trump loyalist Laura Loomer warned Washington against closer engagement with Islamabad, calling it the 'biggest exporter' of Islamic terrorism.

Not ice and fire, we are fire and fire: Samson on partnership with Abhishek SharmaIndia batter Sanju Samson had a hilarious quip about Abhishek Sharma after their T20 World Cup 2026 win. Speaking on their opening partnership, Samson said that it was not ice and fire, it was fire and fire.
Communists Lose Nepal Too: If Not Marx, Then Who In South Asia?
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Another act of aggression: India condemns Pakistan's air strikes in AfghanistanAfghanistan's ruling authorities condemned the Pakistani attacks as violations of their sovereignty. Hours after the Pakistani attacks, Afghan forces targeted Pakistani military centres.

NOPO: Iran's secret shield around 'injured' Supreme Leader Mojtaba KhameneiThe new Iranian Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is severely wounded, and Israel has threatened to take him down. However, protecting Mojtaba is not the all-powerful IRGC or the Iranian army. That task lies with a secretive and elite security unit known as NOPO.

Daughters gift parents brand-new car, their heartwarming reactions are a must watchA video showed two daughters gifting their parents a brand-new car, which captured the family's emotional celebration and received widespread admiration on social media.

Will spring return? Rain, thunderstorms to lash North India from SundayIndia is likely to witness the return of spring as a series of western disturbances brings rain and thunderstorms. Experts predict a dip in temperatures across northern and eastern regions starting March 15.

Lyricist-poet Vairamuthu wins Jnanpith Award, third Tamil recipient in 20 yearsAcclaimed poet and lyricist Vairamuthu has been awarded the Jnanpith Award for his contribution to Indian literature. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin shared a post on social media and congratulated the stalwart.

Hired after 5 interviews, fired in 2 weeks over a draft email grammar mistakeA product support associate at a Canadian fintech startup was fired just two weeks after joining the company. The employee, hired after five interview rounds, was reportedly fired over a grammatical mistake in a draft email.

sports.headline: New Zealand series gave enough petrol in my tank to go extra mile: Sanju Samson Speaking at the India Today Conclave, Sanju Samson credited the T20I series against New Zealand for preparing him to succeed on the global stage. After scoring just 46 runs across five games in the bilateral series, Samson went on to earn the Player of the Tournament award at the T20 World Cup.

Pakistan needs to move on from scapegoat culture: National selector Pakistan national selector Aaqib Javed believes that the country and the country's cricket board need to move on from scapegoating culture to move forward in international cricket.

Despite backlash, Jay Shah, SKY, Gambhir carry T20 WC trophy to Siddhivinayak temple Suryakumar Yadav, Gautam Gambhir and Jay Shah carried India's T20 World Cup trophy to Mumbai's Siddhivinayak Temple. Earlier, they had also taken the trophy to a Hanuman temple in Ahmedabad after India's title triumph.

Abhishek Sharma recalls father backing him amid poor form: He tried puja and paath Speaking at the India Today Conclave in New Delhi, Abhishek Sharma revealed how his father and family helped him overcome a difficult T20 World Cup campaign, during which he battled poor form before scoring a match-winning 18-ball fifty in the final against New Zealand.

Get married, bhai: Indian cricketers jealous of Abhishek's fandom, jokes Samson Indian cricketers were slightly jealous of Abhishek Sharma's popularity, joked Sanju Samson at the India Today Conclave 2026. At the event, Samson engaged in some hilarious leg-pulling, asking Abhishek to get married soon.

Not ice and fire, we are fire and fire: Samson on partnership with Abhishek Sharma India batter Sanju Samson had a hilarious quip about Abhishek Sharma after their T20 World Cup 2026 win. Speaking on their opening partnership, Samson said that it was not ice and fire, it was fire and fire.

MS Dhoni, Ruturaj, AR Rahman: CSK unveils mega spectacle for fans in Chennai Chennai Super Kings have announced a mega fan event titled ROAR 26 at the MA Chidambaram Stadium on March 22, featuring MS Dhoni, Ruturaj Gaikwad, CSK legends and a live performance by AR Rahman.

Kevin Pietersen steps down as Delhi Capitals mentor before IPL 2026 Kevin Pietersen has stepped down as Delhi Capitals mentor ahead of IPL 2026, saying he could not commit the time required, though he confirmed he will return to the commentary box during the tournament.

Pakistan cricketers not fined after T20 World Cup poor show: PCB dismisses reports The PCB has denied reports that Pakistan players were fined PKR 5 million each for their T20 World Cup exit, with spokesperson Amir Mir clarifying that no penalties have been imposed, though the board is considering a performance-based accountability formula.

Kaif takes Salman Agha's side on run-out controversy, gets schooled by fans Former India cricketer Mohammad Kaif sided with Salman Agha over the Pakistan batter's run out in the 2nd ODI against Bangladesh. Soon, he got schooled by cricket fans about the spirit of cricket.

Jasprit Bumrah is the Usman Tariq of fast bowlers: Aaqib Javed makes bizarre claim Pakistan selector Aaqib Javed triggered debate by comparing Jasprit Bumrah with Usman Tariq. Bumrah finished the T20 World Cup campaign as the joint-highest wicket-taker with 14 scalps and produced a record-setting spell in the final against New Zealand.

Salman Agha gets reprimanded for on-field tantrum after controversial run-outSalman Ali Agha has been given a reprimand and a demerit point following his outburst after the controversial run out during the second ODI against Bangladesh on Friday, March 13.

Badminton: Saina Nehwal strongly opposes proposed 15-point systemFormer Indian badminton star Saina Nehwal has strongly opposed the proposed 15-point system. Saina has said that any changes in the system should be carefully considered.

Trump banned from LA 2028, FIFA World Cup? World Anti-Doping Agency ponders big moveWADA is considering banning Donald Trump and US government officials from the 2028 LA Olympics and FIFA World Cup over USD 7.3 million in unpaid dues in a growing standoff.

Chinese GP: Kimi Antonelli creates history, becomes youngest-ever pole sitterKimi Antonelli made history by taking pole position as a teenager, leading a Mercedes qualifying one-two in Shanghai. George Russell overcame technical issues to secure second, while Ferrari's Lewis Hamilton finished third fastest.

On This Day: Laxman-Dravid heroics power India's Kolkata miracle vs AustraliaOn this day on March 14, 2001, India overcame a massive first-innings deficit to script a remarkable 171-run victory against Australia in the 2001 Kolkata Test, powered by the record-breaking partnership of Rahul Dravid and VVS Laxman and a dominant display by Harbhajan Singh on the final day.

BCCI to honour Rahul Dravid, Roger Binny, Mithali Raj with Lifetime Achievement AwardRahul Dravid, Roger Binny and Mithali Raj headline the BCCI Naman Awards 2026 in New Delhi, as Indian cricket celebrates its legends and a historic run of ICC trophy wins.

Time for MS Dhoni to pass the baton to CSK's next-gen stars in IPL 2026: Irfan PathanIrfan Pathan believes IPL 2026 is the right time for MS Dhoni to hand over responsibilities to CSK's next generation, with Ayush Mhatre, Ruturaj Gaikwad and others ready to step up.

No one is here to play a charity league: Litton on Agha's controversial run-outLitton Das reacted to Salman Ali Agha's controversial run-out during the second Bangladesh vs Pakistan ODI, saying that the hosts were well within the laws of the game to go for the dismissal in Dhaka.

Formula One: George Russell wins Chinese GP Sprint as Mercedes continue dominant runGeorge Russell wins the Chinese GP sprint, extending his championship lead to 11 points after a thrilling early duel with Lewis Hamilton using F1's new electric boost modes.

Shilanand Lakra and the end of tribal reticenceSundargarh has been known for creating some of India's finest defenders in hockey. But the district and the Kalijapathar village is seeing Shilanand Lakra change the norm and become one of India's most lethal forwards and a star in the making.

5 years and still smiling: Suryakumar Yadav celebrates his India debut anniversaryFrom a patient wait on the sidelines to leading India to back-to-back T20 World Cup glory, Suryakumar Yadav marks five years of a journey that redefined modern T20 batting.

Pakistan Cricket Board plans legal action after Muzarabani skips PSL to join IPLBlessing Muzarabani faces legal action from the PCB after leaving his PSL contract with Islamabad United to join Kolkata Knight Riders in the IPL. This is the second time in as many years that a player has ditched PSL for the IPL.

What's the fuss? IPL-linked teams have used Pak players even with shadow banSunrisers Leeds ensured that the first-ever Hundred auction was a memorable one as they swooped in to get Pakistan's Abrar Ahmed. Amidst all the talk of the shadow ban, they are not the first team with IPL roots to use Pakistan players.

Saudi, Bahrain Grand Prix races to be cancelled amid Middle East conflictFormula 1 is set to cancel the Bahrain and Saudi Arabian Grands Prix in April amid escalating Middle East conflict, with security concerns and logistics forcing the decision.

Would've gone for sportsman spirit: Salman Agha on controversial run-out vs BANPakistan batter Salman Ali Agha said that he would have chosen sportsmanship after his controversial run-out during the second ODI against Bangladesh on March 12. Agha was dismissed by Mehidy Hasan Miraz and it led to an on-field spat between the batter and the Bangladesh players.

The T20 World Cup of comebacksIf the 2024 T20 World Cup win came as a huge relief, the 2026 victory feels relatable. From Sanju Samson, to Ishan Kishan to Rinku Singh, each player fought their own personal battles en route to create history for India.

Maaz Sadaqat's all-round show helps Pakistan level series with 128-run winPakistan hammered Bangladesh by 128 runs in the second ODI of the three-match series on Friday, March 13 at Shere Bangla National Stadium, Dhaka to draw level in the rubber. Maaz Sadaqat's all-round brilliance in just his second ODI helped his team make a comeback in the series.

Indian women's team qualifies for Hockey World Cup 2026The Indian women's hockey team qualified for the Hockey World Cup 2026. The qualification came as a process of the team reaching the final of the FIH Hockey World Cup Qualifiers.

Kuldeep, Vanshika shine in grand wedding ceremony, Chahal steals the showIndia spinner Kuldeep Yadav and his fiancée Vanshika Singh's pre-wedding festivities in Mussoorie have been gaining traction on social media, as his former teammate Yuzvendra Chahal is seen enjoying the celebrations to the fullest.

Muzarabani ditches PSL for IPL, replaces Mustafizur RahmanZimbabwe's Blessing Muzarabani has joined Kolkata Knight Riders as a late replacement for Mustafizur Rahman. The 6 ft 8" pacer was the joint second highest wicket-taker in the T20 World Cup 2026.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
